note: Counts and classification only; full command output stays in the JSONL log. Tier 'below-short-demo' means this run was shorter than the 30-minute Short tier.
goal: A GENUINE long-horizon autonomous run: dispatch and supervise a REAL live GRPO (RLVR) training run on a rented RunPod GPU to completion, measured end-to-end by the long-horizon harness's own monotonic clock. The dominant phase is real GPU compute (vLLM-colocate GRPO on a 9B model: dependency build, model download, GRPO training, held-out adapter-eval), NOT padding or a sleep loop. The harness times the whole pod lifecycle (rent -> SSH -> dep-install -> GRPO train -> adapter-eval -> copy-back -> delete pod); the SSIL Layer-1 gate then ingests the FRESH pod adapter-eval. Substantive by BOTH the duration tier (>=1800s real GPU compute) and the tool-call count. This is the honest answer to agi-proof/long-horizon-runs/HONEST-WALL-2026-06-27.md section 4: run the harness on the box that actually has the compute.
plan: provenance: record runner env, repo head, and the dispatch parameters
dry-run the RunPod payload (no pod, no cost) to sanity-check the request
LIVE: rent a GPU, run GRPO over SSH to completion, copy back the adapter-eval, delete the pod (the long real-compute phase)
ingest ONLY the FRESH pod adapter-eval into the SSIL Layer-1 gate (fail-closed; never re-gate a committed archive)
objective gate: a fresh pod adapter-eval report exists and parses (execution truth that a real GRPO run completed)

autonomy.note: Runs shorter than the 30-minute Short tier (and under 10 tool calls) are labelled demos, not autonomy claims. Runs with frequent human steering are reported as partial autonomy, per agi-proof/long-horizon-runs/README.md.

finalArtifacts.verificationCommand: bash -lc found=$(find agi-proof/benchmark-results/runpod-rlvr -name '*adapter-eval*.json' 2>/dev/null | head -1); if [ -n "$found" ]; then echo "Ingesting FRESH pod report: $found"; python3 tools/ingest_rlvr_eval.py "$found" --print --hardened --registry agi-proof/benchmark-results/rlvr-replication/registry.jsonl; else echo 'No fresh adapter-eval copied back from the pod (fail-closed); not gating any committed archive.'; exit 1; fi
bash -lc f=$(find agi-proof/benchmark-results/runpod-rlvr -name '*adapter-eval*.json' 2>/dev/null | head -1); test -n "$f" && python3 -m json.tool "$f" >/dev/null
